title: Determining Chlorophyll Concentration using CuSO4 Magnesium-Copper Exchange Titration
author: Victor vmr Rodriguez
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7xrhpm6
materials: Let the raw mix stand for 00:05:00 minutes.
Write the final results as Con ± Moe

steps.title: Add 2 g fof sample plant...
steps.instruction: Add 2 g fof sample plant matter or other test article to a mortar .

steps.title: Add 1 g of Magnesium sulfate...
steps.instruction: Add 1 g of Magnesium sulfate heptahydrate Contributed by users crystals to the mortar.

steps.title: Grind the sample item and Magnesium...
steps.instruction: Grind the sample item and Magnesium sulfate heptahydrate Contributed by users together until the leaves are ground up, the magnesium sulfate is completely dissolved into the plant liquid and a liquid starts to form.

steps.title: Transfer as much of the solid...
steps.instruction: Transfer as much of the solid and liquid slurry to a 20ml test tube.

steps.title: Add 8 mL Acetone Nacalai Tesque Inc
steps.instruction: Add 8 mL Acetone Nacalai Tesque Inc. Catalog #00310-95 to the 20ml test tube. This is the raw mix .

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Acetone Nacalai Tesque Inc
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Acetone Nacalai Tesque Inc. Catalog #00310-95 to the mortar to wash up and collect as much of the sample as possible into the 20ml test tube.

steps.title: Use a glass stirrer to stir the raw mix
steps.instruction: Use a glass stirrer to stir the raw mix . Take care to press the plant slurry down to the base of the test tube, then agitate the slurry again to mix. Repeat this many times for 00:02:00 minutes. Ensure that the acetone is fully incorporated into the slurry.

steps.title: Use a glass stirrer to stir...
steps.instruction: Use a glass stirrer to stir the raw mix again for 00:01:00 minute.

steps.title: Using a 20 micron retention filtration...
steps.instruction: Using a 20 micron retention filtration paper to filter the raw mix into another test tube. Ensure to use a new filter paper for each separate sample. This is the refined mix .

steps.title: Determine the resolution needed for the concentration
steps.instruction: Determine the resolution needed for the concentration. There resolution is the interval of dilution. For example, a 10% resolution will result in a dilution interval of 100%, 90%, 80% etc. The smaller the resolution the narrower the margin of error is. For this protocol, the resolution used will be 10%.

steps.title: Label ten 5ml vials in descending...
steps.instruction: Label ten 5ml vials in descending order from 100% to 10%.

steps.title: Add Acid Activated mix to each...
steps.instruction: Add Acid Activated mix to each 5ml vial at volume based on the formula V ol = 1000ul * P er with P er representing the percentage written on each vial. Use the percentage or decimal, not whole number. Ex. 10% = .10

steps.title: Prepare a 60mg CuSO4 /ml H2O...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 60mg CuSO4 /ml H2O concentration solution of Copper (II) sulfate pentahydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #209198 to be referred to as the copper (II) sulfate Solution .

steps.title: Add 1 mL Hydrochloric Acid Contributed...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL Hydrochloric Acid Contributed by users to the refined mix and mix with clean glass stirrer for 00:01:00 minute to create the Acid Activated Mix .

steps.title: Let the Acid Activated Mix stand for 00:02:00 minutes
steps.instruction: Let the Acid Activated Mix stand for 00:02:00 minutes.

steps.title: Add Acetone Nacalai Tesque Inc
steps.instruction: Add Acetone Nacalai Tesque Inc. Catalog #00310-95 to each 5ml vial at volume based on the formula V ol = 1000ul * (1-P er) with P er representing the percentage written on each vial. Use the percentage or decimal, not whole number. Ex. 10% = .10

steps.title: Gently stir each vial by rotating...
steps.instruction: Gently stir each vial by rotating them in a circular motion for 00:00:30 seconds each.

steps.title: Using an adjustable pipette, dispense 100...
steps.instruction: Using an adjustable pipette, dispense 100 µL of Acid Activated Mix from each vial to the corresponding PCR Tube. Ensure to change the pipette tip for every transfer in order to ensure contamination prevention.

steps.title: Label The proper amount of PCR...
steps.instruction: Label The proper amount of PCR tubes corresponding to the percentages listed on the vials.

steps.title: Add 100 µL copper (II) sulfate...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL copper (II) sulfate Solution to each PCR tube.

steps.title: Close all PCR tubes and Centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Close all PCR tubes and Centrifuge all tubes at 1200 x g for 2 minutes.

steps.title: In a PCR rack, let the...
steps.instruction: In a PCR rack, let the samples stand for 6 hours

steps.title: Centrifuge all tubes at 1200 x g for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge all tubes at 1200 x g for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Observe which PCR is the lowest...
steps.instruction: Observe which PCR is the lowest percentage concentration not to have any trace of precipitated Copper (II) Sulfate . (A blue crystalline precipitate at the bottom. Take note of the percentage number to be used as the variable P -.

steps.title: Assign the highest percentage value vial...
steps.instruction: Assign the highest percentage value vial to have visible copper (II) Sulfate precipitate the variable P + .

steps.title: Use this formula to calculate the...
steps.instruction: Use this formula to calculate the mean value for the concentration in g/ml. (6*10 -4 * 893.51)/(P - * 159.609)+(((6*10 -4 * 893.51)/(P + * 159.609) - (6*10 -4 * 893.51)/(P - * 159.609))/2)= Con

steps.title: Use this formula to calculate the...
steps.instruction: Use this formula to calculate the margin of error of the concentration. ((6*10 -4 * 893.51)/(P + * 159.609) - (6*10 -4 * 893.51)/(P - * 159.609))/2= Moe
